title: 10 best herbal tinctures for stress
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for stress are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, which are used as a natural remedy to help manage stress and promote relaxation. They are made by soaking plant material in a solvent such as alcohol, glycerin, or vinegar, which extracts the active compounds from the herbs. Examples of herbs commonly used in tinctures for stress include chamomile, lavender, passionflower, and ashwagandha. These herbs have calming and soothing properties that can help reduce feelings of anxiety and promote a sense of well-being. By taking herbal tinctures for stress, individuals may experience improved sleep, increased energy levels, and a more balanced mood. For instance, a person who has been struggling with insomnia due to stress may find that taking a chamomile tincture before bed helps them fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer, leading to a more rested and productive day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tincture helps with stress because it contains passive phytoestrogens and antioxidants that can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The active compounds in maypop, such as chrysin and passionflavon, have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate symptoms of stress and promote overall mental well-being. Additionally, maypop has been used traditionally as a sedative and sleep aid, making it a popular choice for those seeking natural relief from the effects of stress and tension. Its soothing properties can help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of calm, making it a valuable tool in managing stress and promoting relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety: Maypop tincture contains active constituents such as passionflower alkaloids and flavonoids, which have been shown to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it an effective aid in managing stress.

Sedative: The sedative properties of maypop tincture, attributed to its harman alkaloids, can help alleviate stress-related symptoms such as insomnia, restlessness, and muscle tension, allowing for better sleep and overall well-being.
Nervine: As a nervine, maypop tincture supports the nervous system, reducing stress-induced irritability, anxiety, and excitability, thereby promoting a calmer and more balanced emotional state.

Adaptogenic: Maypop tincture has adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, enhancing the body's resilience and ability to cope with stressors, ultimately improving overall stress management.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of maypop tincture, due to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can be exacerbated by chronic stress, thus supporting overall stress reduction and well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in soothing compounds, the leaves of maypop tincture plant are commonly used to create a calming tea for stress relief.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, maypop flowers are used in medicinal tea to help combat stress-induced oxidative damage.
Roots: Known for their relaxing properties, maypop roots are a key ingredient in stress-relieving teas, promoting a sense of tranquility.
Seeds: High in essential oils, maypop seeds contribute to the tea's aroma and therapeutic qualities, aiding in stress reduction.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the maypop (Passiflora incarnata) leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests or diseases.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then let them air dry or pat them gently with a clean cloth.
Chop or grind 2 ounces (56 grams) of dried maypop leaves and place them in a clean, dry jar.
Pour 1 pint (473 ml) of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the chopped leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.

Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days. After this period, strain the mixture and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle. Adults may take 1-2 ml up to three times a day for stress relief. Consult a healthcare professional before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tincture helps with stress because it contains active compounds, such as valerenic acid and valepotriates, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds help to regulate the levels of neurotransmitters, such as gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), in the brain. By increasing GABA levels, valerian tincture can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for managing stress. Additionally, valerian tincture has been shown to improve sleep quality, which can also help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Valerian tincture helps reduce anxiety by increasing GABA levels in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress symptoms (active constituents
Sedative: It aids in improving sleep quality and reducing sleep disturbances, alleviating stress-induced insomnia (active constituents
Muscle relaxant: Valerian tincture helps alleviate muscle tension and spasms, which often accompany stress and anxiety (active constituents
Antidepressant: It may help ease depressive symptoms associated with stress by modulating neurotransmitter levels in the brain (active constituents
Neuroprotective: Valerian tincture may protect neurons from stress-induced damage, promoting overall brain health and resilience (active constituents
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain high levels of volatile oils and alkaloids, which are believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them ideal for use in medicinal tea for stress relief.

Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes are underground stems that store nutrients and contain active compounds, including valerenic acid, which has been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic effects, making them a popular choice for medicinal tea to reduce stress and promote relaxation.

Flowers: Valerian flowers contain essential oils and flavonoids, which have been found to have a mild sedative effect, making them a suitable addition to medicinal tea blends designed to alleviate stress and promote better sleep.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried valerian root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped valerian root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent valerian root.
Store the finished valerian tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tincture helps with stress because it contains beneficial compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds, including flavonoids and volatile oils, work together to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Skullcap has been shown to help regulate mood by interacting with neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin. By taking skullcap tincture, individuals may experience a greater sense of calm and well-being, making it an effective natural remedy for managing stress. Its soothing properties can also help improve sleep quality, further reducing the negative impacts of stress on the body and mind.
remedy_properties: Nervine relaxant: Skullcap tincture helps relax the nervous system, reducing tension and anxiety associated with stress due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Adaptogenic: Skullcap tincture supports the body's ability to adapt to stress and promotes overall well-being, thanks to its triterpene and iridoid glycosides constituents.
Anti-inflammatory: Skullcap tincture reduces inflammation in the body, which can contribute to stress-related symptoms, due to its flavonoid, phenolic acid, and iridoid glycosides content.
Antioxidant: Skullcap tincture helps protect cells from damage caused by stress-induced free radicals, thanks to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Sedative: Skullcap tincture promotes relaxation and sleep, alleviating stress-related insomnia, due to its flavonoid and volatile oil constituents.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in soothing compounds, the leaves of the skullcap tincture plant are commonly used to brew calming teas that help reduce stress and anxiety.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, the flowers of the skullcap tincture plant contribute to the plant's stress-relieving properties, making them an excellent addition to medicinal teas.
Stems: Containing therapeutic constituents, the stems of the skullcap tincture plant are used in medicinal teas to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of stress.
Seeds: The seeds of the skullcap tincture plant are a valuable source of beneficial compounds that help combat stress and provide a soothing effect when used in medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried skullcap herb into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out the excess.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for stress relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with stress because it contains powerful compounds like hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to possess stress-relieving properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin, norepinephrine, and dopamine, thereby increasing their availability in the brain and promoting a positive mood. Additionally, St. John's wort has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help protect against the damaging effects of stress on the body. Its soothing properties make it an ideal natural remedy for managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Nervine calming effect: St. John's wort tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety.
Mild sedative properties: The tincture can help promote sleep and reduce insomnia, which are often associated with stress, thanks to its mild sedative properties from hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-depressant properties: St. John's wort tincture can help manage stress-related depression by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, due to its active constituents hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, as hypericin and hyperforin have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can contribute to overall stress reduction.

Adaptogenic properties: St. John's wort tincture can help the body adapt to stress by supporting the adrenal glands, which can become overworked during periods of chronic stress, due to its active constituents including hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which are believed to have mood-lifting and stress-reducing properties.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and phytochemicals, which may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Stems: Contain active compounds, although in lower concentrations than flowers and leaves, that contribute to the plant's overall stress-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and dry, typically in late spring to early summer.
Fill a clean jar with the harvested herb, filling it about 3/4 full, then pour 100 proof alcohol (such as vodka) over the herb until it is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the final St. John's Wort tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. Recommended dosage is 30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed for stress relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats tincture helps with stress because it contains a variety of calming compounds, including nervine herbs that support the nervous system and help reduce anxiety. The tincture is made from oats, which are rich in magnesium, a mineral that helps regulate cortisol levels and promote relaxation. Additionally, oats tincture may help improve sleep quality, which can reduce stress and promote overall well-being. Its soothing properties make it an ideal natural remedy for managing stress and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Oats tincture has a calming and soothing effect on the nervous system, which is important for stress as it can help to reduce anxiety, irritability, and restlessness. This property is due to the presence of nervine alkaloids such as gramine and avenine.

Adaptogenic: Oats tincture helps the body to adapt to stress by normalizing physiological functions, which is important for stress as it can help to improve resilience to stressors and reduce the negative effects of chronic stress. This property is attributed to the presence of beta-glucans and other polysaccharides.

Antidepressant: Oats tincture has mood-enhancing properties, which is important for stress as it can help to alleviate symptoms of depression and low mood that are often associated with stress. This property is due to the presence of avenanthramides and other phenolic compounds.

Anxiolytic: Oats tincture has anxiety-reducing properties, which is important for stress as it can help to reduce feelings of worry, tension, and fear. This property is attributed to the presence of gramine and other alkaloids.

Sedative: Oats tincture has a mild sedative effect, which is important for stress as it can help to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce insomnia that is often associated with stress. This property is due to the presence of alkaloids such as gramine and avenine.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Oat seeds contain compounds that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making them ideal for medicinal tea to alleviate stress.
Leaves: Oat leaves are rich in antioxidants and have calming properties, making them a valuable addition to stress-relieving herbal teas.
Flowers: Oat flowers are known for their soothing effects on the nervous system, making them a popular choice for stress-relief teas.
Stems: Oat stems contain compounds that can help regulate hormonal balance and reduce stress-related symptoms, contributing to the overall effectiveness of medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of dried herbs, such as chamomile, lavender, and oats, and combine them in a clean jar.
Pour 1 quart of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use 1-2 dropperfuls as needed to help manage stress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: California poppy tincture helps with stress because it contains natural compounds that can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The herb has been used traditionally for its calming effects, and recent studies suggest that it may help to regulate neurotransmitter activity in the brain. This can help to alleviate feelings of stress and promote a sense of well-being. Additionally, the tincture form allows for quick absorption into the bloodstream, providing fast-acting relief from stress symptoms. Overall, California poppy tincture can be a natural and effective way to manage stress and promote a more balanced mood.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effect: California poppy tincture can help reduce anxiety, promoting relaxation and alleviating stress symptoms. This is partly due to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids, such as protopine and allocryptopine, which have anxiolytic properties.

Sedative effect: The tincture can help induce sleep and improve sleep quality, which is essential for managing stress. The sedative effect is attributed to the alkaloids, including californidine and sanquinarine.

Nervous system support: California poppy tincture can help support the nervous system, reducing tension and irritability. This is because the alkaloids and flavonoids in the plant have a calming effect on the nervous system.

Pain relief: The tincture can help alleviate pain and discomfort, which can contribute to stress. The presence of alkaloids like protopine and cryptopine contributes to the plant's analgesic properties.
Muscle relaxation: California poppy tincture can help relax muscles, reducing physical tension and stress. The alkaloids and flavonoids in the plant have a relaxant effect on smooth muscles.
remedy_parts: Flowers: California poppy flowers are often used in teas for stress due to their mild sedative properties, which can help promote relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves of the California poppy plant are also used in teas, and they contain flavonoids that may help reduce anxiety and promote calmness.
Seeds: California poppy seeds contain oils that may have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them a useful addition to stress-relieving teas.
Roots: Although less commonly used, the roots of the California poppy plant contain alkaloids that may help alleviate anxiety and promote restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest California poppy flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry jar two-thirds full.
Combine flowers with 100 proof vodka, ensuring flowers are fully submerged, covering with a tight-fitting lid.
Store jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain herbal material from the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any remaining liquid.
Store tincture in a glass dropper bottle, taking 20-30 drops up to three times daily as needed for stress relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tincture helps with stress because it contains compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system. This herb, also known as hops, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The tincture is made by soaking the flowers in alcohol, which extracts the active compounds, including bitter acids and essential oils. These compounds have been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic effects, helping to reduce stress and promote a sense of calm. Additionally, humulus lupulus has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate physical symptoms of stress, such as muscle tension and headaches. Overall, humulus lupulus tincture is a natural and effective way to manage stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Humulus lupulus tincture helps reduce anxiety, which is important for stress as it can help alleviate symptoms such as restlessness, irritability, and muscle tension. The active constituents responsible for this property are the bitter acids and essential oils, particularly humulone and lupulone.

Sedative: The tincture has a sedative effect, which is important for stress as it can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. The active constituents responsible for this property are the same as those responsible for the anxiolytic effect.

Antidepressant: Humulus lupulus tincture has antidepressant properties, which is important for stress as it can help alleviate symptoms of depression that often accompany stress. The active constituents responsible for this property are the flavonoids, particularly xanthohumol.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which is important for stress as chronic stress can lead to chronic inflammation, which can exacerbate stress-related symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are the essential oils and bitter acids.

Antioxidant: Humulus lupulus tincture has antioxidant properties, which is important for stress as chronic stress can lead to oxidative stress, which can damage cells and exacerbate stress-related symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property are the flavonoids, particularly xanthohumol.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Humulus lupulus flowers, also known as hops, are commonly used in herbal teas for stress due to their mild sedative properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the Humulus lupulus plant are sometimes used in tea blends for stress relief, as they contain flavonoids and volatile oils that may have calming effects.
Stems: While not as commonly used as the flowers and leaves, the stems of Humulus lupulus can also be included in tea blends for stress, as they contain similar calming compounds.
Seeds: The seeds of Humulus lupulus are occasionally used in tea formulations for stress, as they contain oils that may have relaxing and calming properties.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Humulus lupulus (hops) flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 5 ounces of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the hops, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the spent hops and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished hops tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. Take 1-2 ml as needed to help manage stress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with stress because it contains active compounds that have a calming and relaxing effect on the body. The main component responsible for these benefits is linalool, a naturally occurring alcohol that has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote sleep. When taken as a tincture, the lavender extract is quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, allowing for fast-acting relief from stress and tension. Additionally, the herbal properties of lavender may help regulate serotonin and dopamine levels in the brain, contributing to an overall sense of well-being and balance. Regular use of English lavender tincture can help manage stress and promote a more relaxed and peaceful state of mind.
remedy_properties: Nervine: English lavender tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety. This is due to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anxiolytic and sedative properties.
Anxiolytic: Lavender tincture can help alleviate anxiety, a common cause of stress. This is attributed to the active constituent linalool, which has been shown to have an anxiolytic effect.

Sedative: English lavender tincture can promote sleep and help reduce stress-related insomnia. This is due to the sedative properties of linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help regulate sleep patterns.

Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tincture can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to stress and anxiety. The active constituent linalool has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties.

Antioxidant: English lavender tincture contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant properties. Reducing oxidative stress can help improve overall mental and physical well-being, reducing the impact of stress on the body.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in soothing essential oils, the flowers of English lavender are commonly used to make medicinal tea for stress relief.
Leaves: Containing calming compounds, the leaves of English lavender can be used to make a soothing herbal infusion to help manage stress.
Buds: High in relaxing constituents, the buds of English lavender are often used in tea blends to promote relaxation and alleviate feelings of stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried English lavender flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the lavender flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the lavender.
Label the bottle with the contents and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for stress relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with stress because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anxiety-reducing effects. This compound works by increasing the levels of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of stress. Additionally, lemon balm has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it an effective natural remedy for managing stress and promoting overall mental well-being. Its soothing properties can also help improve sleep quality, further reducing the negative impacts of stress on the body.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm tincture helps reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for stress management as excessive anxiety can exacerbate stress responses. This property is attributed to the rosmarinic acid and flavonoids present in lemon balm.

Mild sedative effect: Lemon balm tincture aids in improving sleep quality and reducing insomnia, which is crucial for stress relief as poor sleep can increase stress vulnerability. This effect is primarily due to the presence of citronellal and nerol in lemon balm.

Nervine tonic: Lemon balm tincture supports overall nervous system health, which is vital for stress management as a healthy nervous system can better cope with stressors. The active constituents responsible for this property include eugenol, tannins, and terpenes.

Cognitive function enhancement: Lemon balm tincture improves memory, attention, and focus, which is important for stress reduction as it helps maintain mental clarity and prevent cognitive decline under stress. The rosmarinic acid and eugenol in lemon balm contribute to this property.

Adaptogenic property: Lemon balm tincture helps the body adapt to stress by modulating stress response pathways, which is crucial for long-term stress management. This property is attributed to the rosmarinic acid, eugenol, and triterpene acids found in lemon balm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for stress due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have calming effects.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain antioxidants and essential oils that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain some beneficial compounds and can be included in tea blends for stress relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves and stems, chop finely, and place in a clean jar.
Add 1 cup of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring all plant material is covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL as needed for stress relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tincture helps with stress because it contains nepetalactone, a compound that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system. This compound found in catnip binds to receptors in the brain that help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Additionally, catnip tincture can also help to improve sleep quality, which can further reduce stress levels. Its soothing properties make it an ideal natural remedy for those seeking relief from the symptoms of stress and anxiety. Furthermore, catnip tincture is non-addictive and has no known side effects, making it a safe and effective option for long-term use.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Catnip tincture acts as a nervine, helping to soothe and relax the nervous system, which is important for stress because it can help reduce anxiety and promote calmness. The active constituents responsible for this property include nepetalactone, vitamin C, and various volatile oils.

Anxiolytic: Catnip tincture has anxiolytic properties, meaning it can help alleviate anxiety, which is crucial for stress management as it can promote relaxation and emotional well-being. The anxiolytic effects are attributed to nepetalactone and other terpenoids.

Sedative: Catnip tincture exhibits sedative properties, which can help induce sleep and reduce restlessness, making it beneficial for stress relief by promoting better sleep quality and reducing insomnia. The sedative effects are attributed to nepetalactone and other volatile oils.

Antispasmodic: Catnip tincture has antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve muscle tension and spasms, contributing to stress reduction by alleviating physical symptoms associated with stress and anxiety. The antispasmodic effects are attributed to nepetalactone and other iridoid glycosides.

Mild Analgesic: Catnip tincture has mild analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort, making it helpful for stress relief by reducing physical pain that may exacerbate stress and anxiety. The mild analgesic effects are attributed to nepetalactone and other volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nepetalactone, a compound known to reduce stress and anxiety.
Flowers: Contain essential oils and terpenes that promote relaxation and calmness.
Stems: High in antioxidants, which help to manage stress by reducing oxidative damage in the body.
Buds: Packed with active compounds that bind to receptors in the brain, inducing a sense of tranquility.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh catnip herb, place it in a clean jar, and pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over it.
Tightly seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the recommended time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle or jar, label it with the contents and date, and store it in a cool, dark place.
Take 1-2 mL of the catnip tincture up to three times a day as needed for stress relief, or consult with a healthcare practitioner for personalized dosage recommendations.
